body.title: ReaderDevice: node extraction output mode
body.result: node_extractor.py: LLM extraction → {narrative,memory_type,metadata} node dicts; ReaderDevice.read(format=nodes) wired; 20 tests
body.description:
Add format=nodes output mode to ReaderDevice. Generalizes book_learner.py's LLM extraction logic into an extract-only path — caller is responsible for deposit into their learning tree (clan.memories, Scraps embeddings, etc.).

ReaderDevice.read(uri, format='nodes') → list[dict] where each dict matches clan.memories insert shape: {narrative: str, memory_type: str, metadata: {source_uri, chunk_position, book_title?, source_author?, model_used, extracted_at}}. No deposit happens inside the ReaderDevice — the device returns nodes, nothing more.

Extraction prompt generalizes book_learner.py's _extract_nodes: given a chunk, extract 0-N memory nodes (FACTUAL, INTERPRETIVE, PROCEDURAL as appropriate). Keep the qwen-first routing (local LLM, cloud escalation on low confidence).

**Affected files:** devices/reader/node_extractor.py (new — LLM extraction, no deposit), devices/reader/device.py (add format=nodes routing)
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; node shape must be insertable into clan.memories without transformation; no direct Cortex/psycopg2 calls — extraction only; inference via InferenceDevice
**Scope boundary:** extraction only; book_learner.py not retired yet; reading_tool.py not modified; Igor's deposit path (Cortex.store) stays in Igor
**Test plan:** synthetic chunk text → node_extractor → nodes have required fields (narrative non-empty, memory_type in {FACTUAL,INTERPRETIVE,PROCEDURAL}, metadata has source_uri + chunk_position); LLM mocked with fixture response; empty chunk → zero nodes returned
